name: Retinol
lead: The OTC member of the retinoid family. Two enzymatic conversions away from tretinoin, slower to act, gentler on the way in. The honest framing: most people don't need a prescription, they need three months of consistency.

whatItIs.body: etinol is the alcohol form of vitamin A. Inside the keratinocyte it is oxidised first to retinaldehyde, then to retinoic acid — the same molecule prescription tretinoin delivers directly. That two-step conversion is why retinol is slower to act and gentler on the barrier: only a fraction of what you apply ever becomes the active species.
whatItIs.body2: Decades of formulation work have built around that limitation: encapsulation, anhydrous vehicles, and slow-release polymers that protect the molecule from oxidation and meter its conversion. A well-formulated 0.5% can outperform a poorly-formulated 1.0%. Concentration on the label is necessary but not sufficient.

mechanism.b: Binds nuclear retinoic-acid receptors after conversion. Upregulates types I and III collagen, downregulates MMP-1. Visible 12–24 weeks.
Accelerates keratinocyte turnover and disrupts melanosome transfer. Modest but cumulative pigment improvement.
Normalises follicular keratinisation. Useful adjunct for comedonal acne; less powerful than adapalene or tretinoin.

evidence.note: 0.4–1.0% retinol shows reproducible improvement on crow's-feet and forehead lines. Slower than tretinoin, but the curve catches up.
Modest. Better when stacked with niacinamide or vitamin C.
Useful but not first-line; adapalene wins on acne endpoints.
Initial 4–6 wk irritation is the rule, not the exception. Encapsulated forms blunt the curve.

concentration.b: Twice weekly to start. Ramp over 8 weeks.
The sensible default after onboarding. Nightly for most.
Tolerable skin, established routine. Diminishing returns above 1%.
Stability becomes the limit, not skin tolerance.

pairings.note: Buffers irritation and supports barrier overnight. The default pairing.
Compounded irritation with no added benefit. Alternate evenings.
BPO oxidises retinol on contact. AM/PM split or alternate nights.
C in the morning, retinol at night. Antioxidant + remodelling stack.

products.name: Retinol 0.5% in Squalane
products.note: Affordable workhorse. Squalane vehicle limits irritation but also penetration.

products.name: Retinol 0.3 / 0.5 / 1.0
products.note: Reference clinical line. Stepwise ladder, quietly excellent stability.

products.name: Retinol 0.3% Serum
products.note: Indian-market beginner option. Honest dose, simple vehicle.

faq.a: Tretinoin is faster and better-evidenced. Retinol is more accessible and gentler. If you can get tretinoin and tolerate it, that is the ceiling. Retinol is for everyone else — and that is most people.

faq.q: How long until I see results?
faq.a: Eight weeks for tone and texture. Sixteen for fine lines. Twenty-four before you judge collagen endpoints. Retinoids are a discipline, not an event.

faq.q: Why does my skin get worse first?
faq.a: Retinisation. Increased turnover surfaces existing comedones and inflames the barrier. Reduce frequency, support with a ceramide moisturiser, do not abandon the molecule.

faq.q: Can I use retinol while pregnant?
faq.a: No. All vitamin A derivatives — including OTC retinol — are contraindicated. Switch to bakuchiol or azelaic acid.
